entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Farming systems managed by small-scale farmers, focusing on sustainability and holistic design

entities.name: GLOBAL SOUTH
entities.description: Region encompassing developing countries in Africa, Asia, and Latin America where smallholder farming is prevalent

entities.name: SYSTEMS TOOL BOX
entities.description: A comprehensive and user-friendly framework and tool developed to assess the multidimensional sustainability of farming systems

entities.name: MULTIDIMENSIONAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The assessment of sustainability across multiple domains including environmental, economic, productivity, social, and human well-being

entities.name: FARMING AND LIVELIHOOD SYSTEMS
entities.description: The integrated systems of agricultural production and the socio-economic means of living for farmers

entities.name: DEVELOPMENT POLICIES AND INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Strategies and actions aimed at improving farming and livelihood systems, often requiring better targeting

entities.name: AUTOMATED DASHBOARD
entities.description: A digital transformation of the systems tool box designed to promote wider use and accessibility for stakeholders

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The environmental domain used to assess the sustainability of farming systems

entities.name: ECONOMIC SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The economic domain used to assess the sustainability of farming systems

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The productivity domain used to assess the sustainability of farming systems

entities.name: SOCIAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The social domain used to assess the sustainability of farming systems

entities.name: HUMAN WELL BEING
entities.description: The human well-being domain used to assess the sustainability of farming systems

entities.name: SMALLHOLDERS
entities.description: Small-scale farmers whose well-being and farming system sustainability are tracked using the tool
